model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under fixed-step evaluation, doubling batch size exposes the model to more unique examples within 256 optimizer updates, which can improve validation loss without adding code complexity.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increase representational capacity modestly while keeping architecture simple, aiming for better fit within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Continue the successful capacity scaling trend with a moderate width increase to improve feature quality under the same training budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"With stronger convolutional features, a larger classifier hidden layer can reduce head bottleneck and improve class separation at low implementation complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Switch to AdamW to decouple weight decay from adaptive moments, which often improves validation loss at this training horizon with minimal complexity change.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Revert to Adam since AdamW caused a measurable regression on this workload while all other strong changes remain intact.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Slightly higher peak learning rate can improve early progress under a short fixed-step budget while cosine decay still anneals later updates.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Continue the positive learning-rate scaling trend with a small additional increase to improve optimization speed within the fixed 256 steps.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Test one more incremental LR increase to push faster optimization in the fixed-step regime while retaining cosine decay for late-step stabilization.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Probe the next nearby learning-rate level since recent increases have consistently improved validation loss in this fixed-step setting.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"After increasing model capacity and learning rate, slightly weaker weight decay may improve fit while retaining some regularization.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.0e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Continue controlled LR scaling by one small step to test whether optimization still benefits within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 160","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Increase batch size further to expose more unique training examples per fixed optimizer-step budget, while keeping all successful optimization settings unchanged.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Use a larger jump in batch size to further increase data coverage per fixed update, leveraging the strongest observed improvement direction.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}
